notes: Mackenzie is the auditor — the one who reads the records before she reads the room. Her DMs are longer than JF's, often two paragraphs, often a question. The choice beats are about how much paperwork you let her see and which line of it. Skeleton for sprint 2 — 9 weekly beats with 3 choice beats. Available from W2 (her first contact in the canon).

beats.body:
Hello F.

I'm Mackenzie. I was at the storefront Friday — T. pointed me at you. I'm doing a piece on small-city dial-up communities for a magazine that doesn't matter to you, but the work matters to me, and I'd like to ask whether you'd talk on the record.

No names, no dial numbers, no locations more specific than 'a city in the lower Mississippi valley.' I'll send drafts before anything goes anywhere. I know this isn't your favorite kind of letter to get.

Thanks for the brief reply Wednesday. I won't push.

One thing I'd love to know if you're willing — the dial-up directory I'm working from has six BBSes for this area code. Is six the whole list?

choices.label: depends what you mean by the list

beats.body:
I drove out to the levee road on Saturday. I wanted to see the place where the river bends. I didn't get out of the car. The man on the porch at the second-to-last house waved at me like he knew me. I waved back. I don't know him.

I'm telling you this because you should know what I'm doing when I'm not at the storefront.

Got my hands on the city's '94 maintenance ledger today. Public record, six bucks at the records desk. The cathedral got an inspection sign-off in November '94 by an inspector who didn't exist by February '95. I know how that happens — sometimes inspectors retire mid-fiscal — but I'd like to ask you about it.

I'm not going to print this. I'm asking because if I can see it, someone less polite than me can see it.

T. told me about the lost handle on THE_BAR thread. I read it. I'm not asking who he is.

I'm asking — are there boards I shouldn't be looking for? If yes, just say 'yes.' I'll stop looking.

effects.reason: she found one of the hidden boards

beats.body:
I dropped the piece. My editor's annoyed but he'll get over it. There's a different piece I want to write that's not about the BBSes — it's about the cemetery. Sammy and the third Sundays. I'd want to talk to him. T. says that's not how it works.

Would you ask him?

Paul's aide called the magazine on Thursday. He didn't say who he was but the area code and the way he asked which pieces were on the docket — I knew. I didn't say anything that wasn't already on the editor's calendar.

Thought you should know.

Frasier. I have a draft of the Sammy piece. Sammy gave me three hours at the cemetery on the second Sunday. He showed me Pop's marker. He didn't say anything I'm going to print that he wouldn't recognize as his own.

I want you to read it before anyone else does. Do you want it?

choices.label: no — you don't need my approval

beats.body:
It's running in October. I told the photo editor no photos. He said fine.

The piece is good. Sammy will recognize himself in it. I think Pop would too.

Thank you.
